data.title: American Eagle Stock (AEO) Soars as Actress Sydney Sweeney Returns
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) has announced a second advertising campaign with actress Sydney Sweeney, sending its stock up 7% as a result.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The clothing retailer announced that it is partnering with Sweeney for its summer season denim shorts collection that includes low-rise shorts and super low-rise micro "skorts." The first series of advertisements with the actress used the tagline "Sydney Sweeney Has Great Jeans" and faced a social media backlash over claims it promoted eugenics.

American Eagle weathered the controversy and its stock rose 77% in 2025 on the back of the successful ad campaign. Now, the company is looking to replicate that success with a new summer advertising campaign featuring Sweeney, who stars in the hit TV show "Euphoria."

The Power of Advertising

American Eagle also reported that its revenue rose 37% in the six months immediately after the first ad campaign featuring Sweeney was launched. Management said that all the attention from the ad campaign benefitted its financial results and share price.

The new ad campaign is called "Syd for Short: American Eagle Jean Shorts," and it is the latest effort to boost demand for American Eagle's denim products. The company continues to struggle to navigate tariff-related costs as most of its clothing is made overseas, notably in Asia.

Is AEO Stock a Buy?

American Eagle Outfitters' stock has a consensus Hold rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, six Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AEO price target of $24.14 implies 27% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Analysts Are Cautious on Tesla Stock (TSLA) Ahead of Q1 Earnings. Here's Why
data.description:
All eyes are on electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla's (TSLA) first-quarter earnings on April 22. TSLA stock has declined about 13% year-to-date due to concerns about weak deliveries, lack of innovation, and demand challenges amid macro uncertainty and intense competition. Wall Street's consensus rating indicates a cautious stance, with several analysts concerned about the EV business even as Tesla bulls remain optimistic about its full self-driving (FSD) technology, robotaxis, and other AI ventures.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Interestingly, TSLA stock jumped 7.6% on Wednesday, after CEO Elon Musk highlighted the company's progress on its forthcoming AI5 chip. In a post on his social media platform X, Musk said that the AI5 chip has reached a key engineering milestone and is getting closer to production.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Tesla to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.37 for Q1 2026, reflecting 37% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise 15.4% to $22.32 billion from the prior-year quarter.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Allbirds Stock (BIRD) Rockets on Plans to Shift from Footwear to AI
data.description:
Allbirds (BIRD) stock underwent a massive rally on Wednesday after the footwear company announced that it would shift to the AI sector. The company revealed that it has reached an agreement with an institutional investor for a $50 million convertible financing facility. Allbirds will use the funds from this agreement to facilitate its transformation into an AI compute infrastructure company. Its long-term goal is to become a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Allbirds said that the agreement with the institutional investor is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026. The company also said that its changes in operations will come alongside a name change. This will see Allbirds change its name to "NewBird AI."

Allbirds also noted that it has signed an agreement with American Exchange Group for the sale of its footwear business. American Exchange Group intends to continue to build the Allbirds brand following this acquisition. In connection with this, Allbirds plans to grant shareholders a special dividend. It estimates that this will be payable in Q3 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 20, 2026.

Allbirds Stock Movement Today

Allbirds stock was up 238.15% on Wednesday, extending an 80.98% year-to-date rally. However, the stock has fallen 45.63% over the past 12 months.

News of Allbirds' change into an AI company attracted extra investor attention today. As of this writing, more than 7.8 million shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 54,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Investors Cheer as Costco (COST) Raises Its Dividend 13%
data.description:
Retail giant Costco Wholesale (COST) has increased its quarterly dividend by 13%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Moving forward, Costco will pay its stockholders a quarterly dividend of $1.47 per share, a 13.1% increase from the previous distribution of $1.30. On an annual basis, Costco shareholders will earn $5.88 a share. The increased dividend will be payable on May 15 of this year to stockholders of record on May 1. The ex-dividend date is April 30.

The increase brings the annual dividend yield on COST stock to 0.60%. Costco has now raised its dividend for 22 consecutive years. The company is known for consistent dividend growth, with a 1,200% total increase since 2004. Costco is on the cusp of becoming a Dividend Aristocrat, which are companies that have increased their dividend payouts for 25 consecutive years or longer.

What About the Special Dividend?

Costco Wholesale also has a history of paying special dividends to shareholders every few years. In 2017, the company paid a one-time dividend of $7 per share; in 2020 it paid a special dividend of $10 a share; and in 2023 a special dividend was declared that was worth $15 per share.

Shareholders are wondering when Costco will next pay a special dividend, which are issued in addition to the quarterly distributions. Some analysts attribute the slide in COST stock over the past six months to the fact that management has not announced another special dividend payment. Special dividends are typically used to return excess cash to shareholders.

Is COST Stock a Buy?

Costco's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 23 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average COST price target of $1,097.28 implies 11.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Jumps as Xbox Mulls a Return to Exclusive Games
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) stock climbed on Wednesday alongside recent rumors of a strategy change at video game subsidiary Xbox. According to recent insider reports, Xbox is considering dropping its multi-console release strategy and returning to exclusive releases for its video game consoles. This would follow years of financial trouble for the video game company that forced it to try new methods to generate revenue.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

If the latest rumors are true, Microsoft hasn't benefited enough from its multi-console strategy to keep it up. This makes sense as its release of in-house games on rival consoles, such as Sony's (SONY) PlayStation 5, have diminished the value of the Xbox brand.

Fortunately, Xbox has plenty of development studios under its control. A spree of acquisitions over the past several years has brought Elder Scrolls developer Bethesda, Call of Duty maker Activision, and World of Warcraft developer Blizzard under its control. With these purchases came a hefty number of outstanding IPs. If Microsoft takes these series exclusive, it could make a compelling argument for gamers to buy Xbox consoles again.

Microsoft Stock Movement Today

Microsoft stock was up 3.54% on Wednesday, but was still down 15.7% year-to-date. However, the stock was still up 5.79% over the past 12 months.

MSFT stock trading activity today was muted, as some 15 million shares changed hands, which is roughly half the company's three-month average daily trading volume.
data.author: William White

data.title: Cadence and Nvidia (NVDA) Team Up to Train Robots Faster with AI
data.description:
Software firm Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) is teaming up with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) in order to develop artificial intelligence for robotics. The partnership brings together Cadence's expertise in chip design software with Nvidia's AI capabilities, with both CEOs highlighting the goal of speeding up how robots are trained.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

The key part of the partnership is combining Cadence's physics engines with Nvidia's AI models. These physics engines simulate how real-world materials and objects behave, which helps create realistic training environments. When paired with Nvidia's AI, robots can learn in computer simulations rather than only in the real world. As a result, robots can be trained faster and in a more controlled way, while still learning how to handle real-life situations.

Interestingly, this isn't the only way Nvidia is working on improving robotics. In fact, earlier this year, it released an open-source, AI-focused platform called Alpamayo that's designed to support Level 4 autonomous vehicle development. Similar to the Cadence partnership, it combines simulated and real-world AI datasets to improve how autonomous vehicles handle safety and navigation, especially in complex or less common driving situations.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 41 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average Nvidia price target of $273.57 per share implies 38.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Netflix (NFLX) Is About to Report Q1 Earnings Tomorrow. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is scheduled to announce its first-quarter results after the market closes on Thursday, April 16. The stock has gained about 10% year-to-date, driven by strong ad growth, early U.S. price hikes, and a solid content lineup. The stock also got a boost after the company walked away from a merger with Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), along with a $2.8 billion breakup fee.

data.title: Top BofA Analyst Raises Apple Stock (AAPL) Price Target Ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) stock is down 2% year-to-date due to concerns about higher input costs, tariffs, and the impact of macro uncertainty on consumer spending. Nonetheless, ahead of Apple's Q2 FY26 earnings, AAPL bulls are optimistic about the stock, citing several positives, including a loyal iPhone customer base and innovative offerings like the new MacBook Neo. Interestingly, Top Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan reiterated a Buy rating on AAPL stock and raised his price target to $325 from $320.

Mohan expects the company to report solid results, driven by strength in iPhone sales and the Services business.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Apple to report EPS (earnings per share) of $1.93, reflecting 17% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise more than 14% to $108.92 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat as Investors Pause after Strong Market Rally
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady on Wednesday evening as the market paused following a massive relief rally. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.02% and 0.03%, respectively, at 6:06 p.m. EDT on April 15, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were up 0.07%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

In the regular trading session, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite hit fresh intraday and closing records, with the indices rising 0.8% and 1.6%, respectively, as investors looked past the Iran war. The S&P 500 closed above 7000 for the first time, while the Nasdaq finished above 24,000 after an 11th straight gain. The Dow slipped 0.2%.

Looking ahead, key economic reports include the Industrial Production and Capacity Utilization data points for March and the Weekly Jobless Claims. On the earnings front, TSMC (TSM), Abbott (ABT), Netflix (NFLX), and PepsiCo (PEP) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
